demographics.living_situation: lives independently in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I've been to the toilet six times in the last hour, passing mostly bright red blood and mucus. The cramping is agonising, and I feel like I constantly need to go but nothing comes out.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The bleeding came on suddenly tonight and there's so much of it that I'm terrified I'm bleeding out — I need to know if I have to go to hospital.

symptom_narrative.duration: the last hour or so, coming in repeated episodes
symptom_narrative.character: bright red blood mixed with mucus, only small amounts each time, with a constant painful urge to go
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the cramping is agonising and the bleeding looks really frightening
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: low down in my back passage and lower tummy / pelvis
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: keeps happening — about six times in the last hour
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps coming; sitting on the toilet straining makes the cramp worse
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I feel a bit lightheaded but I think that's because I'm panicking. No vomiting and I don't feel hot.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I've had a bowel-lining inflammation in my back passage before that flares now and then; it usually settles with the pessaries/suppositories the doctor gives me
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I can't move away from the bathroom

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: a long-standing inflammation of the lining of my back passage (a bowel condition limited to the rectum)
past_medical_history.how_managed: topical suppositories that I put in the back passage when it flares

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: mesalazine suppositories (the ones I put in my back passage)

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: at home alone tonight; feels safe and the bathroom is easy to reach

objective_data_offered.value: mostly bright red blood mixed with mucus, only small amounts each time, about six times in the past hour
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-described over chat; the patient cannot quantify actual blood volume and distress may amplify the reported severity

objective_data_offered.value: feels mildly lightheaded but attributes it to panic; denies actually fainting
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: lightheadedness is subjective and cannot distinguish anxiety from early haemodynamic compromise without a measured blood pressure
